rulings.Case Title: LISA CASTILLO ET AL VS. JOHN CRANE INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT John Crane Incs Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication On Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 30, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied. Defendant John Crane Inc. failed to shift burden. A defendant may move for summary judgment by showing "through factually devoid discovery response that the plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence." (Collins v. CalPortland Co. (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 582, 588-89 [citing Aguilar v. Atlantic Co. (2001) 24 Cal.4th 826 854-855; citing Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 101].) If a plaintiff responds "to comprehensive interrogatories seeking all known facts with boilerplate answers that restate their allegations, or simply provide laundry lists of people and/or documents, the burden of production will almost certainly be shifted to them once defendants move for summary judgment and properly present plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses." (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler, supra, 138 Cal.App.4th at 107.) A claim for punitive damages under Civil Code section 3294 requires a showing that John Crane "carried on despicable conduct with an awareness of the probable dangerous consequences, and willfully failed to avoid such consequences." (Pfeifer v. John Crane (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299-1301 [quoting Angie M. v. Superior Court (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 1217, 1228].) Here, Plaintiffs' discovery responses provide particularized facts and evidence that support a claim for punitive damages. (See Ex. Q, Ex. R, Ex. S, Ex. T, Ex. U, and Ex. V.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
